title: rubus idaeus subsp. idaeus
intro: Rubus idaeus subsp. idaeus, also known as the wild strawberry plant, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and fruits of Rubus idaeus subsp. idaeus have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, offering numerous health benefits. Rubus idaeus subsp. idaeus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
